title: Mudroom Bench with Drawers
summary: A six-foot entry bench with three real drawers on full-extension slides — 2x4 frames wrapped in plywood, with a face frame that makes it read as cabinetry.
description:
This bench is a gateway build: it looks like cabinetry, but underneath it's two 2x4 ladder frames stood on edge, skinned in plywood, with drawer boxes riding full-extension slides between them. If you've been screwing 2x4 furniture together and want to graduate toward cabinets, this is the project that teaches drawers without asking you to build a carcass.

The plan's own emphasis is the right one: the two frames must match EXACTLY, because the drawer slides bridge between them — a quarter inch of mismatch and the drawers rack and bind forever. Build the second frame on top of the first. Equally load-bearing advice: the drawer cut list says CUT TO FIT in capital letters. Read your specific slides' instructions, measure your actual openings, and build the boxes to those numbers; the listed dimensions assume standard 1/2"-clearance side-mount slides and true 3/4" stock.

The finishing move — 1x2/1x4 face frame on the cabinet, 1x3 frames on the drawer fronts with an even 1/8" reveal — is what turns shop-grade materials into a piece that lives in the front hall. Take time on those reveals; they're the whole first impression.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood sheet into the two 23-7/8" strips — a straightedge guide is near-mandatory.
Face frames and (optionally) the 2x4 frames and drawer boxes.
Face frames, drawer bottoms, and the top.
Setting the drawer slides dead level on their 2x4 spacers.

materials.name: Plywood, 3/4" cabinet grade, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into two 96" x 23-7/8" strips — sides and top.

materials.name: 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: The two frames and the slide spacers.

materials.name: Full-extension side-mount drawer slides
materials.note: Buy FIRST and read their instructions — the drawer boxes are built to the slides, not to the plan.

cutList.note: CUT TO FIT your slides and openings — listed sizes assume 1/2"-clearance slides.
Cut to fit — match your slide length.
Approximate — sized to an even 1/8" reveal.

cutList.part: Drawer face frame stiles, end drawers
Drawer face frame stiles, middle drawer

steps.title: Build two IDENTICAL 2x4 frames
steps.body:
Cut the frame parts and assemble two ladder frames — 3" self-tapping screws, or 1-1/2"-setting pocket holes with 2-1/2" pocket screws.

Build the second frame stacked on top of the first, corner to corner. The drawer slides span between these frames; 'identical' isn't a virtue here, it's a functional requirement.

steps.title: Skin the ends
steps.body: Attach the plywood side panels to the frame ends — 2" screws, or nails and glue. The bench silhouette appears.

steps.title: Build the drawer boxes — to your slides, not the list
steps.body:
Read the slide instructions first, then build three boxes to fit your openings: for standard 1/2"-clearance side-mounts, the box is the opening width minus 1", sides as long as the slides.

Pocket-holing the boxes? Put the front pocket holes INSIDE the box (the drawer face frame hides interior faces later is not true — the outside face shows through the opening). Bottoms go on with glue and 3/4" brads or narrow-crown staples.

steps.title: Mount the cabinet slide members
steps.body: Set 2x4 scraps inside the bench as spacers, rest each cabinet-side slide member on its spacer, and screw to the frame. The spacer trick guarantees left and right slides sit at identical heights — which is the entire game with drawers.

steps.title: Mount the drawer members and hang the boxes
steps.body: Attach the drawer-side slide members to the boxes per the slide instructions, and set each box into its cabinet slides.

steps.title: Tune the fit
steps.body: Cycle every drawer fully. Binding or rubbing gets fixed NOW — slide screws loosened, shimmed, re-driven — because after the face frames go on, access gets worse and motivation gets weaker.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Fasten the plywood top with 1-1/4" brads and glue (screws hold better but show — your call on a piece this visible). Ease the front edge well; the backs of knees meet it every shoe-tying.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Pocket-hole the 1x2/1x4 face frame together flat on the bench (3/4" setting, 1-1/4" screws), then attach the completed frame to the front with brads and glue. Building it as a unit first is why the reveals come out even.

steps.title: Face the drawers
steps.body: Build the three 1x3 drawer face frames and attach each to its box with brads and glue, holding an even 1/8" gap on all sides — playing-card stacks make good shims. Add the knobs, finish the whole piece, and reclaim the front hall.

images.alt: Six-foot mudroom bench with three drawers on full-extension slides
